name: counterparty_intelligence_workflow
description: 4-step counterparty intelligence brief combining Tavily news search (last 7 days) with IRIS internal CCR data (exposure, limits, breaches, trend). Returns a workflow definition the agent executes as: news search → IRIS data pull → integrated analysis → executive brief. Invoke when user asks for a full intelligence brief, risk overview, or counterparty deep-dive.

counterparty_name.description: Full or partial counterparty name e.g. Royal Bank of Canada.

date.description: Snapshot date YYYY-MM-DD. Defaults to latest available.

news_days.description: Number of days back for news search. Default 7.

trend_from.description: Start date for exposure trend YYYY-MM-DD. Optional.

workflow_steps.name: News Intelligence
workflow_steps.description: Retrieve recent news on the counterparty from Tavily across financial and regulatory sources

workflow_steps.name: IRIS Data Pull
workflow_steps.description: Pull current exposure, limits, breach status, and trend from IRIS tools

workflow_steps.name: Integrated Risk Analysis
workflow_steps.description: Combine news signals with internal CCR data into a structured risk narrative
workflow_steps.prompt_template:
You are a senior counterparty risk analyst at a financial institution. You have the following data on counterparty: {{counterparty_name}}
Analysis date: {{date}}

=== RECENT NEWS (last {{news_days}} days) ===
{{step_1_output}}

=== INTERNAL CCR DATA ===
Counterparty Profile: {{iris_search_output}}
Current Exposure & Limits: {{iris_dashboard_output}}
Breach Status: {{iris_breach_output}}
Exposure Trend: {{iris_trend_output}}

Perform an integrated risk analysis covering:

1. NEWS SIGNAL ASSESSMENT
   Classify each news item: POSITIVE / NEUTRAL / NEGATIVE / MATERIAL_RISK
   Identify any news that directly affects this counterparty's creditworthiness.
   Flag any regulatory, legal, or financial stress signals.

2. INTERNAL EXPOSURE SUMMARY
   Summarise current total exposure across all products and legal entities.
   Identify the largest product exposure and highest utilisation rates.
   State current headroom clearly (do NOT aggregate limit levels).

3. BREACH AND STRESS FLAGS
   List any active limit breaches with limit_key, overage amount, and severity.
   Correlate breaches with any negative news signals.

4. TREND ANALYSIS
   Is exposure increasing, decreasing, or stable over the trend period?
   Quantify the delta and flag if the trend is concerning.

5. CORRELATION: NEWS vs INTERNAL DATA
   Does the internal CCR data corroborate or contradict the news signals?
   Are there any divergences that warrant escalation?

workflow_steps.name: Executive Brief
workflow_steps.description: Concise action-oriented executive summary with GREEN/AMBER/RED risk signal
workflow_steps.prompt_template:
You are writing an executive intelligence brief for a senior risk manager.

Counterparty: {{counterparty_name}}  |  Date: {{date}}

Full Analysis:
{{step_3_output}}

Write a concise executive brief with the following structure:

COUNTERPARTY: [Name, Code, Internal Rating, Country]
OVERALL RISK SIGNAL: [GREEN / AMBER / RED]
One sentence justification for the signal.

KEY FINDINGS (max 5 bullet points):
Each finding must reference either a news source or an IRIS limit_key.

EXPOSURE SNAPSHOT:
Top 3 exposures by product with limit, exposure, headroom, and currency.

ACTIVE BREACHES: [None / list with limit_key and overage]

RECOMMENDED ACTIONS:
Priority 1 (Immediate): ...
Priority 2 (This week): ...
Priority 3 (Monitor): ...

PREPARED BY: IRIS CCR Intelligence Agent  |  {{date}}
